figures.caption: Table 1: Cross-embodiment retrieval and PHASOR ablation. Each query has one positive, the temporally identical chunk from another embodiment (R→R over ( 4 2 ) =6 pairs, %). (A) varies the loss at a fixed target, adding LAMP soft targets at the coarse clip, fine window, and both levels. (B) varies the head input at the best loss, Xroot vs. our pooled zpose. Bold best, underline second.

Figure 3: Left. 2D PCA of the alignment embedding z (top) and phase manifold P(τ) (bottom). Same clip across the human and four robots overlaps tightly (col. 1), while G1 separates four motion types (walk, run, jump, kick) by orbit radius and position (col. 2). Right. Phase shifts ∆S on a running clip yield predictable temporal offsets, each row at a different gait phase.
Figure 1: Structural gaps of PAE. (a,b) Whole-body (A,F ) conflates distinct motions (walk vs. wave), which per-part features resolve. (c) Velocity-based (A,F ) ignores posture difference.

Figure 2: Overview of PHASOR. (a) A frozen human phase encoder, shared across embodiments via per-robot adapters, yields a morphology-invariant manifold P(τ); a per-embodiment pose branch couples in only at the decoder via bidirectional FiLM. (b) Manifolds are projected onto S63 and aligned by soft InfoNCE (LAMP prior) plus speed and shape consistency terms.

Figure I: Effect of the LAMP mixing ratio λmix. Clip-level (left) and exact-match (right) mAP over λmix ∈ {0.0, 0.3, 0.7, 1.0} for all three directions. Both peak at 0.3 (our default) and collapse at 1.0 when the hard target is removed.

Table I: Body-part partition and channel allocation. We enforce a consistent five-part semantic structure and phase channel allocation (Kb) across heterogeneous embodiments. J denotes total joints, and Dk is velocity dimension (3J). The 3D root linear velocity is concatenated to the trunk (TK) input.

Table II: Explicit joint index mappings. Joints are assigned to the five semantic parts following each embodiment’s specific URDF hierarchy. Left/Right symmetric parts are grouped for brevity, and the numbers in parentheses indicate the degrees of freedom (e.g., pitch, roll, yaw) for that joint group.
Table IV: Hyperparameter configuration for both training stages.

Table V: Cross-embodiment clip-level retrieval on the DPAE val set (2,472 clips). H→R: human query → robot gallery (avg. over G1/H1.2/T1/BHL). R→H: robot query → human gallery (avg. over G1/H1.2/T1/BHL). R→R: robot ↔ robot (avg. over ( 4 2 ) =6 pairs, both directions). All values in %. Bold: best per column. Underline: second best.
